steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content: The subject is the emission of light, a singular noun phrase. The long which-clause only describes the wavelength; it does not change the need for a singular finite verb.

steps.title: Reject nonfinite forms
steps.content: Choice A is a present participle, Choice C is an infinitive, and Choice D is a perfect participle. None can serve as the main verb of the clause.

steps.title: Choose subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Identifies is a singular finite verb that agrees with emission and completes the sentence's main clause. The answer is B.
